Tomato___Early_blight.description: Early blight is a common fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani. It affects leaves, stems, and fruit, causing dark concentric rings and yellowing.
Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Brown spots with concentric rings on older leaves, yellow halos, leaf drop, stem lesions, fruit rot near stem.
Tomato___Early_blight.causes: Warm humid weather, poor air circulation, infected plant debris, overhead irrigation, stressed plants.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Crop rotation, remove infected debris, mulch soil, water at base, ensure spacing for airflow, use resistant varieties.
Tomato___Early_blight.prescription: Apply chlorothalonil or copper fungicide every 7-10 days. Use mancozeb or azoxystrobin. Remove severely infected leaves. Improve nitrogen balance.

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Late blight is a devastating disease caused by Phytophthora infestans. It spreads rapidly in cool, wet conditions and can destroy entire crops.
Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked gray-green lesions on leaves, white fuzzy growth underside, brown lesions on stems, firm brown spots on fruit.
Tomato___Late_blight.causes: Cool moist weather, infected seed potatoes, wind-borne spores, overcrowding, prolonged leaf wetness.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant certified disease-free seed, avoid overhead watering, destroy volunteer plants, use resistant cultivars, monitor weather alerts.
Tomato___Late_blight.prescription: Apply chlorothalonil, copper, or phosphorous acid fungicides immediately. Use mandipropamid or fluopicolide for severe cases. Remove and destroy infected plants.

Potato___Early_blight.description: Early blight in potatoes is caused by Alternaria solani, producing characteristic target-spot lesions on foliage and reducing tuber yield.
Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown lesions with concentric rings, yellowing and defoliation from bottom up, tuber dark lesions with corky texture.
Potato___Early_blight.causes: Alternaria spores from crop residue, warm temperatures, alternating wet/dry periods, nutrient stress especially nitrogen.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops 3-4 years, hill soil around plants, balanced fertilization, irrigation management, certified seed tubers.
Potato___Early_blight.prescription: Fungicides: azoxystrobin, chlorothalonil, or mancozeb. Apply at first sign. Supplement with potassium. Store tubers properly after harvest.

Potato___healthy.description: The leaf appears healthy with no significant disease symptoms. Continue good agricultural practices to maintain plant health.
Potato___healthy.symptoms: Uniform green color, no spots or wilting, normal leaf texture and venation.
Potato___healthy.causes: N/A - plant is in good health under proper care conditions.
Potato___healthy.prevention: Maintain soil pH 5.0-6.0, adequate irrigation, balanced NPK fertilization, pest monitoring, crop rotation.
Potato___healthy.prescription: No treatment needed. Continue preventive care: organic compost, drip irrigation, integrated pest management.

Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial spot is caused by Xanthomonas species. It creates small water-soaked lesions that become necrotic and reduce fruit quality.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small dark raised spots on leaves, yellow halos, fruit scab-like lesions, leaf drop in severe infections.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.causes: Infected seeds, splashing rain, high humidity, warm temperatures 24-30°C, handling wet plants.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified disease-free seed, avoid working wet fields, copper spray preventively, rotate away from solanaceous crops.
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.prescription: Copper hydroxide or copper sulfate sprays. Streptomycin where permitted. Remove infected plants. Apply Actigard plant activator for resistance boost.

Tomato___healthy.description: The tomato plant leaf shows no disease indicators. The plant is thriving under current conditions.
Tomato___healthy.symptoms: Vibrant green leaves, intact margins, no necrosis or chlorosis.
Tomato___healthy.prevention: Regular monitoring, proper staking, consistent watering, calcium supplement to prevent blossom end rot.
Tomato___healthy.prescription: No chemical treatment required. Apply balanced fertilizer 10-10-10 monthly during growth. Mulch and prune suckers as needed.

Pepper__bell___healthy.description: Healthy bell pepper foliage with optimal photosynthetic capacity and no pathogen activity detected.
Pepper__bell___healthy.symptoms: Deep green glossy leaves, firm texture, no lesions or curling.
Pepper__bell___healthy.prevention: Well-drained soil, full sun 6-8 hours, avoid overwatering, companion planting with basil or marigold.
Pepper__bell___healthy.prescription: Maintain with organic matter compost. Light feeding with low-nitrogen fertilizer during fruiting.

default.description: Analysis completed. Please consult local agricultural extension for specific guidance.
default.causes: Environmental and pathogen factors may vary by region.
default.prevention: Regular monitoring, proper irrigation, crop rotation, resistant varieties.
default.prescription: Consult certified agronomist for region-specific treatment recommendations.
